annotations.label: Erlangen program

anchor.snippet: the Erlangen program is a method of characterizing geometries based on group theory

annotations.note: The Erlangen program as a methodological framework is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hierarchy of geometries

anchor.snippet: Euclidean geometry was more restrictive than affine geometry

annotations.note: No formal hierarchy comparing Euclidean/affine/projective symmetry groups exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group of symmetries of a geometry

anchor.section: The problems of nineteenth century geometry
anchor.snippet: With every geometry, Klein associated an underlying group of symmetries

annotations.note: Mathlib has specific symmetry groups (isometries, affine equivs) but no abstract notion of 'group of symmetries of a geometry' in the Kleinian sense.

annotations.label: Invariants under Euclidean vs projective groups

anchor.section: The problems of nineteenth century geometry
anchor.snippet: lengths, angles and areas are preserved with respect to the Euclidean group of symmetries

annotations.note: Mathlib proves isometries preserve distances and angles, but does not frame this comparatively against the projective group.

annotations.label: Affine subgroup of projective group

anchor.section: The problems of nineteenth century geometry
anchor.snippet: the group of affine geometry is a subgroup of the group of projective geometry

annotations.note: No embedding of an affine group into a projective group is recorded in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Traditional spaces as homogeneous spaces
annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a 'homogeneous space' definition realizing the classical geometries as G/H.

annotations.label: Projective geometry symmetry group

anchor.snippet: the group of projective geometry in n real-valued dimensions is the symmetry group of n -dimensional real projective space

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Projectivization` but does not characterize its symmetry group.

annotations.label: Affine group as stabilizer of hyperplane at infinity

anchor.snippet: The affine group will be the subgroup respecting

annotations.note: No formalization of the affine group as the stabilizer of the hyperplane at infinity exists.

annotations.label: Parallelogram is affine, circle is not

anchor.snippet: being a parallelogram is affine since affine transformations always take one parallelogram to another one

annotations.note: Mathlib has no parallelogram predicate or theorem about its affine invariance.

annotations.label: Euclidean group as semidirect product

anchor.snippet: The Euclidean group is in fact (using the previous description of the affine group) the semi-direct product of the orthogonal

annotations.note: Mathlib has the semidirect product construction but doesn't express the Euclidean group as O(n) ⋉ ℝⁿ.

annotations.label: Motion of a geometry

anchor.snippet: an element of the group is sometimes called a motion of the geometry

annotations.note: No notion of 'motion' as a group element acting on a geometry is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ultraparallel theorem via hyperbolic motions

anchor.snippet: Such a development enables one to methodically prove the ultraparallel theorem by successive motions

annotations.note: The ultraparallel theorem of hyperbolic geometry is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Oriented elliptic vs oriented spherical geometry

anchor.section: Abstract returns from the Erlangen program
anchor.snippet: oriented spherical geometry (the same non-Euclidean geometry , but with opposite points not identified) have isomorphic automorphism group

annotations.note: No formalization of elliptic vs oriented spherical automorphism groups exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elliptic geometries with different curvatures

anchor.section: Abstract returns from the Erlangen program
anchor.snippet: elliptic geometries with different radii of curvature have isomorphic automorphism groups

annotations.note: Elliptic geometry is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex, dual and split-complex numbers as SL(2,R)/H

anchor.section: Abstract returns from the Erlangen program
anchor.snippet: Complex , dual and double (also known as split-complex) numbers appear as homogeneous spaces

annotations.note: Mathlib defines complex and dual numbers but does not realize them as SL(2,ℝ)/H homogeneous spaces.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic, de Sitter and inversive geometries share automorphism group

anchor.section: Abstract returns from the Erlangen program
anchor.snippet: n -dimensional hyperbolic geometry , n -dimensional de Sitter space and ( n −1)-dimensional inversive geometry all have isomorphic automorphism groups

annotations.note: Neither de Sitter space nor inversive geometry is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Anti-de Sitter and conformal Lorentzian space

anchor.section: Abstract returns from the Erlangen program
anchor.snippet: n -dimensional anti-de Sitter space and ( n −1)-dimensional conformal space with "Lorentzian" signature

annotations.note: Anti-de Sitter and Lorentzian conformal spaces are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Covering group of SU(2,2) isomorphic to covering of SO(4,2)

anchor.section: Abstract returns from the Erlangen program
anchor.snippet: The covering group of SU(2,2) is isomorphic to the covering group of SO(4,2)

annotations.note: Indefinite signature Lie groups SU(2,2) and SO(4,2) and their covering groups are not formalized in Mathlib.
